Title: Integrative Dissection of Lignin Composition in Tartary Buckwheat Seed Hulls for Enhanced Dehulling Efficiency

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 274 Tartary buckwheat accessions; Atcomt1 mutant background
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to seed hull mechanical properties and dehulling efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 5-hydroxylated coniferyl intermediates to produce precursors for S lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cumulative COMT expression is much higher in stem than in leaf or seed hull
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with seed hull S/G ratio; function validated in Arabidopsis stem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown Tartary buckwheat during 2018 and 2019 cropping seasons
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursors of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch step toward S lignin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for S lignin branch; supported as a dedicated enzyme affecting S/G ratio
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes S lignin accumulation and increases S/G ratio, contributing to softer seed hulls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; selective sweep analysis; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous complementation in Arabidopsis Atcomt1; lignin monomer profiling by thioacidolysis-GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtCOMT1 was located in a selective sweep during Tartary buckwheat breeding. 2. FtCOMT1 was significantly associated with seed hull S/G ratio by GWAS. 3. Expressing FtCOMT1 in Arabidopsis Atcomt1 restored S lignin and reduced 5-OH-G accumulation to wild-type levels, confirming conserved COMT function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FtCOMT1 clusters with AtCOMT1 in phylogenetic analysis, contains five conserved COMT regions, and shares 66% and 66.8% amino acid identity with AtCOMT1 and PtCOMT1, respectively
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising breeding target to increase S/G ratio, soften seed hulls, and enhance dehulling efficiency in Tartary buckwheat
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (no accession reported); GWAS using 1,444,061 SNPs; selective sweep analysis using population polymorphism data

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to seed hull mechanical properties and dehulling efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely methylates 5-hydroxylated monolignol intermediates in S lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Included among three putative COMTs; cumulative COMT expression was higher in stem than other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown Tartary buckwheat during 2018 and 2019 cropping seasons
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursors of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch step toward S lignin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes S lignin biosynthesis; selected during domestication/breeding
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtCOMT2 was identified as another putative COMT involved in S lignin biosynthesis. 2. FtCOMT2 was selected in comparisons between wild species and more domesticated varieties. 3. SNP and haplotype frequency differences across subpopulations support breeding selection at this locus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional complementation or GWAS association was shown for FtCOMT2 in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for modulating S lignin content during breeding
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (no accession reported); selective sweep analysis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to seed hull mechanical properties and dehulling efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates coniferaldehyde to 5-hydroxyconiferaldehyde in the S lignin branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Five putative F5H copies identified; cumulative F5H expression is much higher in stem than in leaf or seed hull
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown Tartary buckwheat during 2018 and 2019 cropping seasons
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch step toward S lignin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme for S lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes S lignin accumulation; selected during breeding/domestication
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Five putative F5H genes were identified in Tartary buckwheat. 2. Their cumulative expression was highest in stem, consistent with higher S lignin there. 3. One putative F5H locus showed evidence of selection between wild species and cultivars, implicating S lignin biosynthesis in breeding.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not specify which F5H copy was the functionally prioritized core locus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to elevate S lignin and reduce seed hull hardness
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (no accession reported); selective sweep analysis
